title: Managed Web/API Hosting on Azure App Service
description: Deploy APIs and background workers on Azure App Service with automatic scaling, deployment slots for zero-downtime releases, and built-in CI/CD integration. Supports Windows and Linux runtimes, custom domains, and App Service Environment for vnet isolation.

cost.costNotes: Free tier (F1) includes 60 CPU minutes/day and 1GB disk; suitable only for development/testing. Basic tier (~$13/month) required for always-on production apps.

provenance.estimatedMonthlyRangeUsd: Min $0 represents Azure App Service Free tier (F1) with 60 CPU minutes/day, 1GB RAM, and 1GB disk - suitable only for development/testing or very low-traffic hobby projects. Max $150 represents a production setup with Basic B2 tier (~$55/month for 2 cores, 3.5GB RAM) or Standard S1 tier (~$70/month for 1 core, 1.75GB RAM with staging slots) plus additional costs for deployment slots ($10-20), custom domains/SSL, and Application Insights monitoring ($20-40). For typical small to medium API workloads, expect $13-70/month range (Basic B1 to Standard S1). Scaling to Standard S2 or S3 for higher traffic would push costs to $140-280/month, but this estimate caps at $150 for a single production instance with monitoring.
provenance.adoptionCost: Medium-high adoption cost driven by Azure-specific concepts and operational maturity requirements. Team needs to learn: (1) Azure Portal navigation and resource management ($200 equivalent in learning time), (2) App Service deployment methods including ZIP deploy, Git integration, and Azure DevOps pipelines ($300 for CI/CD setup), (3) Configuration management with app settings, connection strings, and Key Vault integration ($150), (4) Deployment slots and traffic routing strategies ($100), (5) Monitoring with Application Insights and log analytics ($50). While App Service abstracts infrastructure, production-ready deployment requires understanding health checks, scaling rules, and Azure networking concepts. AI coding assistants can help with deployment scripts and configuration templates, reducing what would be $1200-1500 down to $800.
provenance.licenseCost: Azure App Service is a platform service with no separate licensing fees. Costs are purely consumption-based through the hosting plan tier selected (Free, Shared, Basic, Standard, Premium, Isolated). No proprietary software licenses, IDE requirements, or third-party tools are mandatory. Optional tools like Visual Studio Enterprise or Azure DevOps advanced features have separate costs but are not required - VS Code (free) and basic Azure DevOps pipelines (free tier available) are sufficient for deployment.

provides.reasoning: Azure App Service provides managed hosting for web applications with automatic infrastructure management, making this a core capability of the pattern.
The pattern explicitly supports API deployment with managed infrastructure, scaling, and deployment features.
Pattern description mentions background workers as a supported workload type on App Service.
Azure App Service provides automatic horizontal and vertical scaling capabilities, which is a key feature of this managed platform.
Deployment slots are explicitly mentioned and are a native App Service feature for blue-green deployments and staging environments.
Built-in CI/CD is mentioned in the description, enabling automated deployment pipelines.
As a managed PaaS, this pattern reduces operational overhead by handling infrastructure management, patching, and maintenance.
App Service includes automatic SSL/TLS certificate management and free managed certificates for custom domains.
App Service includes built-in load balancing across instances when scaled to multiple instances.
App Service includes built-in health checks and monitoring capabilities for application availability and performance.

requires.reasoning: App Service requires deployable application code (web app, API, or worker) to host.
Azure App Service requires an active Azure subscription to provision and operate.

availability.reason: Azure App Service is publicly available

supports_nfr.reason: Azure App Service Free/Basic tier single-region deployment provides up to 99.9% availability without multi-region redundancy.
Managed PaaS introduces network overhead and shared infrastructure resulting in minimum p95 latency of 50ms.
Tail latencies on shared infrastructure with cold starts and resource contention require p99 of at least 200ms.
Deployment slots enable rapid rollback achieving minimum RTO of 2-3 minutes via traffic swap.
Free tier CPU minute limitations and Basic tier scaling constraints limit peak throughput to 360K jobs/hour.
Free/low-cost App Service tiers support moderate read query throughput up to 1000 QPS before requiring premium tiers.
Free/low-cost App Service tiers support moderate write query throughput up to 500 QPS before requiring premium scaling.
Azure App Service Easy Auth supports OAuth2/OIDC/SAML, and applications can implement API key, JWT, mTLS, or password authentication.
Azure App Service in EU regions supports GDPR compliance with data residency controls when needed.
Premium/Isolated App Service tiers support HIPAA compliance with BAA when required, though free/basic tiers do not.
Azure infrastructure and App Service support CCPA compliance requirements when necessary.
Azure audit capabilities and App Service monitoring support SOX compliance controls when required.

supports_constraints.reason: Azure App Service is exclusively available on Microsoft Azure cloud platform.
Azure App Service natively supports these runtime stacks with built-in language support and deployment tooling.
Pattern hosts web applications, REST APIs, and background workers serving any client platform.
Pattern can host applications with or without caching; caching is optional application-level concern.
Pattern supports hosting message consumers/producers or synchronous APIs without messaging requirements.
Pattern can host AI inference APIs or non-AI applications depending on workload requirements.
Pattern supports single-tenant and multi-tenant application architectures depending on design.
Free/low-cost App Service tiers have limited CPU minutes unsuitable for intensive batch processing workloads.
Free tier instability and resource constraints make it unsuitable for distributed transaction coordination.
Free tier CPU minute limits and resource constraints prevent continuous real-time streaming workloads.
Pattern can host APIs that integrate with external vector search services or non-vector applications.
Hosting pattern is agnostic to backend data store choice including document databases.
Pattern can host applications using key-value stores or other storage types.
Pattern supports hosting applications that integrate with graph databases or other data stores.
Pattern can host time-series APIs or non-time-series applications depending on use case.
Pattern supports hosting transactional APIs or read-heavy workloads.
Pattern can host analytical query APIs though free tier may have resource limitations for heavy OLAP workloads.

tier.description: App Service pricing tier. Free (F1): 60 CPU min/day, 1GB disk, no custom domains. Basic: $13+/mo, always-on, custom domains. Standard: auto-scaling, deployment slots. Premium: enhanced performance and VNet integration.

auto_scaling.description: Enable automatic horizontal scaling based on CPU/memory metrics. Requires Standard tier or higher. Trade-off: cost vs. automatic traffic handling.

deployment_slots.description: Enable staging slots for blue-green deployments and testing before production swap. Requires Standard tier or higher. Reduces deployment risk but increases cost.

always_on.description: Keep app loaded even when idle to avoid cold starts. Requires Basic tier or higher. Trade-off: faster response times vs. higher cost.

runtime_version.description: Runtime version strategy. Latest: newest features but potential breaking changes. LTS: long-term support for stability. Specific: pin to exact version for maximum control.

warn_nfr.reason: At very low read QPS, managed PaaS overhead (network latency, cold starts, shared infrastructure) may not be justified compared to simpler serverless options.
warn_nfr.message: hosting-managed-paas--azure: peak read QPS is {actual} req/s (<5). Managed App Service overhead may not be justified; consider serverless Functions for sporadic traffic.

warn_nfr.reason: At very low write QPS, the always-on nature of App Service (even in Free tier) may be overkill compared to consumption-based serverless compute.
warn_nfr.message: hosting-managed-paas--azure: peak write QPS is {actual} req/s (<2). Always-on App Service may be overkill; consider consumption-based serverless for sporadic writes.
